title: Ludovico Einaudi
review: Einaudi is often a contemporary entry point into classical music, but when you're used to hearing world-class orchestras perform Brahms or Shostakovich, he can feel underwhelming by comparison. Granted, there were a few moments that stole my breath, but most of it felt more like background music: partly by design, given how repetitive his melodies are and how often they turn up in films and ads. His compositions are also intimate, so a huge arena like Ziggo flattens the layering, making it harder to pick out the individual musicians' technical work. That said, the venue's scale allowed for a striking lighting display and the hushed atmosphere over a crowd that size felt all-encompassing. Yet the number of phones recording, despite requests not to, said something about the audience's unfamiliarity with classical concert etiquette. Grateful for my Ticketmaster resale miracle, I would not have been happy paying 100+ for it.
